skill.name: blockrun
skill.description: Enables AI agents to access capabilities they lack by routing requests to external models (GPT, Grok, Gemini) with micropayments via crypto wallet
skill.summary: Pay-per-use SDK for cross-model AI calls with wallet-based micropayments for image generation, real-time X data, and alternative LLM opinions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 64 patterns that are false positives: markdown code block delimiters misidentified as shell backticks (58), hidden directory path in documentation (4), and no actual crypto code present (2). The skill is documentation for a legitimate cross-model AI SDK using wallet-based micropayments. External command and filesystem risks are from documented Python SDK usage patterns, not executable malicious code.

content.user_title: Route AI tasks to external models with crypto micropayments
content.value_statement: Access capabilities your AI lacks like image generation and real-time X data. Pay per token with crypto wallet instead of managing API keys.
actual_capabilities: Route requests to external LLMs (GPT, Grok, Gemini, DeepSeek) via x402 micropayments
Generate images using DALL-E or Nano Banana models
Access real-time X/Twitter data via xAI Live Search integration
Track per-call spending with budget limits and balance checks
Support multiple AI providers without managing individual API keys
limitations: Requires crypto wallet funding with USDC on Base network
Per-token costs accumulate and require active budget monitoring
Real-time X data requires explicit search parameters to activate
Cannot execute external models without user wallet authorization

use_cases.title: AI Developer needing second opinions
use_cases.description: Route code reviews or complex reasoning tasks to GPT-5.2 while working primarily in Claude Code. Get alternative perspectives on architecture decisions.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using AI coding assistants

use_cases.title: Social media manager requiring live data
use_cases.description: Access real-time X/Twitter posts and trending topics via Grok Live Search when your primary AI lacks live internet access.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and social media managers

use_cases.title: Content creator needing image generation
use_cases.description: Generate images for blog posts or social media directly within your AI workflow using DALL-E integration without switching tools.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: blockrun generate an image of [describe your image]. Use DALL-E for photorealistic results.

prompt_templates.title: Real-time X Data Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: use grok to check what [handle or topic] is posting on X right now. Enable live search.

prompt_templates.title: Second Opinion on Code
prompt_templates.prompt: blockrun second opinion with GPT on this code: [paste code]. Review for bugs and suggest improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Code review with alternative AI perspective

prompt_templates.title: Budget-Controlled Multi-Model Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use blockrun with max $2 budget. First check my balance, then use Grok with live search to research [topic], summarize findings with GPT-5.2, and generate a cover image with DALL-E. Report total spending.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-step task with spending control

output_examples.input: blockrun generate an image of a sunset over mountains
output_examples.output: Image generated successfully via DALL-E. URL: https://oaidalleapiprodscus.blob.core.windows.net/... | Cost: $0.04 | Wallet balance: $4.96 USDC

output_examples.input: use grok to check what @blockrunai posted today
output_examples.output: Found 3 recent posts from @blockrunai: [1] 'New x402 integration live' (245 likes), [2] 'Base network deposits now supported' (128 likes), [3] 'Grok-3 pricing updated' (89 likes). Sources: 3 | Cost: $0.075
Wallet: 0x742d35Cc6634C0532925a3b844Bc9e7595f0bEb | Balance: $5.23 USDC on Base network | Total spent this session: $0.12 across 4 calls

best_practices: Set explicit budget limits before starting multi-step tasks to prevent unexpected charges
Use max_search_results parameter with Grok Live Search to control per-query costs
Check wallet balance before large operations and monitor spending after each call
Choose cost-appropriate models: DeepSeek for simple tasks, GPT-5.2 for complex reasoning
anti_patterns: Making repeated Live Search calls without setting max_search_results - costs accumulate quickly at $0.025 per source
Using expensive models like GPT-5.2 for simple tasks that DeepSeek could handle at fraction of cost
Forgetting to report spending to users after completing paid operations - always show transaction summary
Calling external models for tasks Claude can handle natively - adds unnecessary cost and latency

faq.question: How do I fund my BlockRun wallet?
faq.answer: Run setup_agent_wallet() to generate your wallet address and QR code. Send USDC on Base network to the displayed address. Minimum recommended: $1-5 for testing.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed my budget?
faq.answer: BlockRun tracks spending per session. When budget is reached, it stops making calls and notifies you. You can set a new budget or continue manually.

faq.question: Do I need separate API keys for each model provider?
faq.answer: No. BlockRun uses x402 micropayments from your wallet to pay providers directly. One wallet works across OpenAI, xAI, Google, and DeepSeek.

faq.question: How much does a typical operation cost?
faq.answer: A 500-word GPT-5.2 prompt costs ~$0.001 input. A DALL-E image costs $0.04. Grok Live Search costs $0.025 per source (default 10 sources = $0.25).

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. BlockRun is designed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It extends capabilities these AIs lack like image generation and real-time X data.

faq.question: Where is my wallet data stored?
faq.answer: Wallet credentials are stored in $HOME/.blockrun/.session on your local machine. This is a standard hidden directory for CLI tool configuration.
